name: Monstera obliqua - Bolivia Form
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Bolivia Form' is a cultivated locality form originating from Bolivia. It is a delicate climber found in humid, lowland forests, typically inhabiting shaded understories. This form is known for its slender climber habit with elongated, asymmetric leaves that are thin and delicate. It features a pronounced oblique base and a narrow tip, with large, irregular inner perforations.

fieldNotes.title: Southern Range Obliqua
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A Bolivian locality form of Monstera obliqua from the southern end of the species' natural distribution range. Bolivia is not where most collectors imagine obliqua when they picture its native range, but the species extends further south than the Peruvian and Colombian material that dominates collector discussion. At ultra rare pricing; genuinely uncommon. Locality forms allow collectors to build a georeferenced collection of a single species, which is a specific and interesting collecting approach that the mainstream hobby has not yet fully adopted.
